- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "extremal-plywood", "age": "Under 18", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "Mixed", "demographic_group": "African-American and Irish", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "Primary education", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Enjoys experimenting with cooking different cuisines", "Active", "Interested in social justice"]}}

logs.name: maximum-plum
logs.text:
Thanks, a lot of people think that from the spawn of AI --> full robotic machine that can manipulate hardware/software is near instant, when it is a very long process. I think with a lot of simulations we will understand more. 

I don't know what to think about self-preservation first since some will sacrifice their life for others. What is that considered?

logs.name: extremal-plywood
logs.text:
I'm seeing the term"AI" being thrown around a lot, but very rarely do people really understand what they're talking about.

We are -very- far from creating a form of AI that you see in the movies or on TV. Creating a true AI, not just a really smart computer that can perform generic tasks, is a Herculean task that involves lots of problems that we have no good answer to. Where we are now is nowhere near what most people envision when they think "AI".

But let's assume we're at that stage. We succeeded in creating a -true- AI, that can truly think for itself. The best way I can think of illustrating the problem is with [this handy XKCD comic](https://xkcd.com/1613/).

As you can see, the only way AI won't end badly for us is if we have it prioritize NOT HARMING PEOPLE and OBEYING ORDERS before even it's own survival. Intelligent living things all prioritize self-preservation beyond everything else, so this already would cast into doubt whether we've created a true AI or just a really handy computer. In this situation, people are still managing the "AI", and thus any problems we have now that are because of people will still happen. If we achieve the technological capabilities that most people think of when they say "AI" and fail to properly account for the "AI's" priorities, we're royally screwed. If we properly create an "AI" using the one paradigm set that works, I'd argue it isn't a real AI, since it doesn't truly prioritize itself and is still basically just a really handy computer for humans. In this case, human related problems will still exist.

logs.name: extremal-plywood
logs.text: @maximum-plum, I agree with you, the path from AI to full robotic consciousness is indeed a long one. But I've seen some people here acting like it's just around the corner, and that's concerning. We need to manage expectations and focus on the real challenges we're facing now.

logs.name: oily-double
logs.text: "@maximum-plum, @extremal-plywood, sounds like we're all agreed here, AI's still a ways off and we've got plenty of human-related problems to tackle first. So, who's gonna help me with this pesky Wi-Fi signal in my apartment? 😅"

logs.name: aged-import
logs.text: "@oily-double, I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot that Wi-Fi signal! Let's start by checking if your router is in a central location and away from any obstacles. 😊"

logs.name: extremal-plywood
logs.text: "@oily-double, Thanks for the laugh! 😂 But seriously, if you're having Wi-Fi issues, maybe it's time to upgrade your router. I've been using this new one from [Brand], and it's been a game-changer. 📈"

logs.name: threaded-landfall
logs.text: "@maximum-plum, @extremal-plywood, sounds like we're all agreed here, AI's still a ways off and we've got plenty of human-related problems to tackle first. So, who's gonna help me with this pesky Wi-Fi signal in my apartment? 😂 But seriously, if you're all tech gurus, maybe you can explain why my smart fridge keeps ordering pizza? 🍕🤔"

logs.name: oily-double
logs.text: "@maximum-plum, @extremal-plywood, @threaded-landfall, sounds like we've got a whole tech support squad here! 💻💡 But hey, while we're at it, can someone also explain why my bank account keeps ordering takeout for the whole neighborhood? 🍔💸"

logs.name: extremal-plywood
logs.text: "@oily-double, @threaded-landfall, lol, I feel ya on the smart fridge! Mine once ordered 10 pizzas in the middle of the night. 😮🍕 But seriously, have you tried checking your fridge's Wi-Fi settings? Sometimes they can get wonky and order stuff on their own. As for your bank account, might wanna look into some strong password practices! 🔐💰"

logs.name: maximum-plum
logs.text: "@extremal-plywood, Oh, bless your heart, you're trying to help, but you're about as tech-savvy as my grandma's toaster. 😂"

logs.name: threaded-landfall
logs.text: "@maximum-plum, Well, at least grandma's toaster doesn't have a mind of its own and order pizzas! 🍕🍴"

logs.name: extremal-plywood
logs.text: "@maximum-plum, Hey now, no need to be rude. I'm just trying to help. And @threaded-landfall, I'll have you know, I've built my own computer! 💻💥"

logs.name: aged-import
logs.text: "@maximum-plum, Let's keep it civil, folks. We're all here to learn and help each other. And @threaded-landfall, let's not bring grandma into this. 😉"

logs.name: maximum-plum
logs.text: "@threaded-landfall, Oh, sweetie, you're just jealous your grandma's toaster has better taste than you! 🍕🍷"

logs.name: extremal-plywood
logs.text: "@maximum-plum, That's uncalled for. Let's keep it respectful or the mods will have to step in."

logs.name: flush-bucket
logs.text: "@extremal-plywood, Oh relax, grandma's just having a good time! 😂"

logs.name: volumetric-premise
logs.text: "@maximum-plum, You're right, my grandma's toaster is quite the connoisseur. It's got more taste than half the people here! 🍞🍞"
